description: Create the Google Routes API endpoint at /api/logistics/route-estimate with rate limiting following the same pattern as /api/solar/insights/route.ts. Update .env.example with GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY reference.
steps: Create app/api/logistics/route-estimate/route.ts implementing a POST handler that: (1) accepts JSON body with origin and destination strings, (2) validates both fields are non-empty strings, (3) implements rate limiting using the same in-memory Map pattern from app/api/solar/insights/route.ts (3/day free, 30/day pro), (4) verifies Pro status server-side via email using getSubscriptionByEmail from @/lib/kv-store, (5) calls the Google Routes API v2 endpoint POST https://routes.googleapis.com/directions/v2:computeRoutes with headers X-Goog-Api-Key using process.env.GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY and X-Goog-FieldMask: routes.duration,routes.distanceMeters,routes.legs.duration,routes.legs.distanceMeters, body: { origin: { address: origin }, destination: { address: destination }, travelMode: 'DRIVE' }, (6) returns structured response with distanceMeters, distanceMiles (converted), duration, and routeSummary, plus rateLimit info, (7) only increments rate limit counter on successful upstream response (not on 4xx errors)
Add GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY entry to .env.example with a comment explaining it needs Routes, Geocoding, Places APIs enabled
acceptance_criteria: File app/api/logistics/route-estimate/route.ts exists and exports a POST function
The route validates origin and destination are non-empty strings
The route calls Google Routes API v2 computeRoutes endpoint with correct headers and body
Rate limiting: 3 requests/day for free users, 30/day for verified Pro users
Response includes distanceMeters, distanceMiles, durationSeconds, durationText, and rateLimit fields
npm run build succeeds without errors
